author: gurukamath
content:
One nuance worth deliberating over at this stage is what happens if a type 4 transaction has enough gas to pay for all the authorizations at the run time level but OOGs due to one of the other gas charges at this level (it's recipient is non-existent for example). In that case, do we roll back the set authorizations or not?

Option 1 - We roll back. This is better in terms of protocol simplicity (we don't have to create 2 different OOG handling semantics at the run time level) and is consistent with the line we have taken so far - the user has to make sure they have included enough gas for what they want to do in the tx.

Option 2 - We don't roll back. This is consistent with current 7702 semantics - once the tx has enough gas for authorizations, it gets the authorizations done.

I'm leaning towards option 1 just from a simplicity perspective but curious to hear what others think
